description: Genie 3 and Genie 2 from DeepMind represent a major breakthrough in building large-scale foundation world models that enable AI systems to understand and predict physical dynamics. These models are critical for training embodied AI agents without requiring extensive real-world interaction.

Waymo's world model represents a specialized application of foundation models for autonomous driving simulation and perception. This enables safer testing and deployment of self-driving technology at scale.

topic: Multimodal Robotics with Large Language Models
description: Gemini Robotics and similar systems integrate LLMs with robotics to enable more capable and generalizable robot behavior. This bridges the gap between language understanding and physical world action.

Academic and industry research into whether and how LLMs learn world models versus surface statistics is accelerating, with tools like V-JEPA 2 and diffusion-based approaches advancing the field. This foundational research shapes how future physical AI systems will be built.

YC-backed startups like Vassar Robotics are democratizing robot arms with sub-$1000 hardware that can learn new skills. This hardware accessibility is crucial for scaling robotic learning across more organizations.

Ex-Waymo engineers launching Bedrock Robotics with $80M to automate construction, alongside other robotics startups, signals massive capital influx into physical AI applications. This reflects investor confidence in near-term commercialization of robotics.

The OpenUSD alliance (Pixar, Adobe, Apple, Autodesk, Nvidia) is establishing open standards for 3D digital representations critical for simulation and world modeling in robotics. This infrastructure layer enables broader ecosystem development.

Open-source tools like MuJoCo physics simulator provide foundational infrastructure that researchers and roboticists use to build and test embodied AI. These tools are essential building blocks for world model training and robot control.
